faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal absent from Taxi-to-Driver API (3.2.2). Both UC 1.1 Task 4 and UC 1.5 Task 4 require the central to confirm an accepted order to the driver. The central sends Confirm to the taxi module (3.4.1), but 3.2.2 provides no Confirm signal for the taxi module to forward this to the driver.

No Central-to-Taxi signal to inform the driver that no taxis are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires this notification so a new order with a different time can be submitted, but no such signal is defined in 3.4.1.
Order_struct contains no priority field. Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized — some customers are more important than others — but the data structure defines no priority attribute.
No signal for the system to send a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires this estimate, but 3.3.2 defines no corresponding Central-to-Operator signal.
LoginSignal defined without parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send driver identification and position to the central, but LoginSignal carries no data and cannot convey this information.
LoggedIn(integer) carries only taxi number. Task 3 requires driver information to be transmitted to the central, but the signal has no driver ID parameter.
LoginFailed signal absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid driver card and keep the car in Offline state, but 3.4.1 defines no LoginFailed signal.

faults.use_case: 1.4 Taxi: Log in / 1.9 Taxi: View traffic overview
faults.description: ZoneInfo absent from Central-to-Taxi API. UC 1.4 Task 6 requires the central to proactively send zone overview after login; UC 1.9 Task 2 requires it in response to an Overview request from the driver. Neither is possible without ZoneInfo in 3.4.1.
No cancellation signal from Central to Taxi after 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires automatic order cancellation, but 3.4.1 defines no cancellation notification signal.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal to communicate the Waiting-for-Customer state to the central. Task 6 requires the car to enter this state after accepting an order, but no Taxi-to-Central signal for this state transition exists in 3.4.2.

MSC 4.1 missing Central-to-Taxi Confirm after step 8 (Ack received). UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order to the driver, but no Confirm step appears in the MSC after the Ack.

Position(integer, integer) missing a time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send both arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central, but Position carries only taxi number and zone number.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal in Taxi-to-Central API. UC 1.8 Tasks 1-2 require the taxi to alert the central and open a voice channel when the driver activates the alarm, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

No alarm activation signal in User-to-Taxi API. UC 1.8 Task 1 requires the driver to activate the alarm via the terminal; all terminal input goes through 3.2.1. No alarm activation signal exists there; Reset_Alarm covers only resetting, not activation.

Position(integer, integer) transmits zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires exact coordinates sent to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm, but the signal provides only a coarse zone identifier.

No Overview request signal in Taxi-to-Central API. Task 1 requires the driver to request traffic overview, which must be forwarded by the taxi module to the central, but 3.4.2 defines no such forwarding signal. Table 1 includes OverviewSignal (Id 9), confirming it was intended but omitted from the API.

No signal to notify the operator when a designated car rejects a manually dispatched order. Variant 4b requires operator notification for reassignment, but no rejection notification is defined in 3.3.2.

Text_Message signal has no group mechanism. Task 3 requires sending to a specific car, a group, or all cars. The signal supports only one car (by number) or all cars (parameter 0) — no group addressing is possible.
No text message acknowledgment signal in Taxi-to-Central API. Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge receipt to the central, but no such signal is defined in 3.4.2.
No signal to notify the operator of failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b requires this notification, but no corresponding signal exists in 3.3.2.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal in Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 requires operators to authenticate with user ID and password, but 3.3.1 contains no signal for submitting login credentials.
Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is in 3.3.1 (Operator-to-Central) but described as a response signal. Response signals belong in 3.3.2 (Central-to-Operator).

Voice_msg in 3.4.2 has 2 parameters (taxi number, voice data), but MSC 4.2 step 16 shows Voice_Msg with 3 parameters (Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data). The operator number is missing from the Taxi-to-Central signal definition.
Confirm_Voice missing from User-to-Taxi signals (3.2.1). MSC 4.2 step 5 shows the driver sending Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) to the taxi module, but this signal is not defined in 3.2.1.
No signal for the driver to request properties (3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver property information (3.4.1). UC 1.16 requires both request and response, but neither is defined.

faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal (defined in 3.4.1, sent Central-to-Taxi over the radio link) is absent from communication protocol Table 1. All radio-link signals must be encoded in Table 1, but no Confirm message type exists there.
No signal for updating driver properties in Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 Tasks 2-3 require the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no such signal.
